resources.string: Hide native subtitles / focus on target language
Drag within the white line to adjust
• Press and drag the block to move
• Drag the white corner to resize
• Drag the white right edge to adjust width
• Drag the white bottom edge to adjust height
After enabling, drag the window to block native subtitles for bilingual video learning.
First time: allow overlay permission, then tap Enable.
Choose close button position, sound effects, and keep-alive.
Follow system language or choose a fixed language below.
Import/export JSON via clipboard for reuse across devices
Learn the learning scenario and what each setting does
Update check failed. Try again later.
You already have the latest version
You chose to be reminded on a newer version
You will be reminded when a newer version appears
No APK asset found. Opening release page.
Unable to open installer. Please install manually.

Current version: %1$s Latest version: %2$s
Download and install now?

Use case: when watching bilingual videos, block native-language subtitles while keeping target subtitles or the picture to support language learning.

Main actions:
• Enable Blocking: show the blocker and drag/resize it
• Disable Blocking: hide the blocker
• Overlay permission: required for first use

Settings:
• Close button position: choose where the close button appears
• Sound effects: toggle interaction sounds
• Keep alive in background: keep a foreground notification to reduce system cleanup
• Transparency toggle: temporarily make the blocker transparent to view target subtitles or picture
• Auto-restore after transparency: restore blocking automatically
• Auto-restore seconds: how long transparency lasts
• Language: follow system or lock to a language
• Import/Export: save or restore settings via clipboard
